item.title: Burger bombs
item.description: Garlic Parmesan Cheeseburger Bombs Ingredients: 1 lb ground beef 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese 1/2 cup cream cheese, softened 1 tsp garlic powder 1 tsp onion powder Salt and pepper, to taste 1 can (16 oz) refrigerated biscuit dough 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese 2 tbsp butter, melted Fresh parsley, chopped (for garnish) Instructions: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. In a skillet over medium heat, brown the ground beef until fully cooked. Drain any excess grease. Add garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Mix in the cream cheese and shredded cheddar cheese until well combined, then remove from heat. Open the can of biscuit dough and separate it into individual biscuits. Flatten each biscuit into a circle. Place a spoonful of the beef and cheese mixture in the center of each flattened biscuit. Fold the dough over the filling and pinch the edges to seal completely. In a small bowl, mix melted butter and grated Parmesan cheese. Brush the tops of the cheeseburger bombs with the garlic Parmesan mixture. Arrange the bombs on the prepared baking sheet and bake for 12-15 minutes, or until golden brown. Let the bombs cool slightly before serving. Garnish with fresh parsley for a pop of color! Enjoy these delicious, cheesy bites with a touch of garlic and Parmesan!
item.dc:creator: Sensiblyinteresting

item.dc:creator: Headcase444

item.title: Transition Timeline 10 yrs/ thriving
item.description:
10 years ago I was ready to leave this life, having been born with a brain that did not match my body… I decided to give myself one last chance and seek help.
This is me today. No filters, just ten years of MTF HRT and the drive to live my best life. This is the first time in my life I have felt happy, and whole, and like myself.

item.description:
greetings online friends
what kind of hornet/wasp (?) is that?
mmm hmm
reasons
they say i never do one for the ladies
i love the castle and pirate ones from my era. but havent they re-released some?
i will defend bic to the death, 1 of their lighters lasts me months and i smoke a good bit of weed
how to fold ingredients
accurate
wait what
i would die for cheezit
nope
no thank you
gross
he finally found inner peas
i would've wanted that as a kid just for the gun
yeah. all of those
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: I might be in trouble…
item.description:
I’ve not been feeling well since the end of September. I had a bad cold, that turned into a sinus infection and then upper respiratory infection by mid-October. The URI cleared up for the most part, but it appeared to be lingering just enough to make it hard to breathe. Without insurance and very little work recently, I didn’t have the money to go to the E.R. and my new doctor couldn’t schedule me for my first appointment until mid-November. So, I told myself that I just needed to tough it out for a few more weeks and I’d be able to get some antibiotics to evict this infection. I made it to my doctor appoint on Thursday morning, the doctor took one look at me & my list of symptoms and told me to go straight to the E.R. He called ahead for me and 20 minutes later I was in a room in the E.R. with tests being run. The last two and a half days have been stressful af! Initial monitoring indicated very high blood pressure and liquid in & around my lungs and heart. An ultrasound of my heart and kidneys showed reduced functionality in both, with my heart being weak flow and my kidneys struggling. They put me on diuretics and BP medication. My BP was north of 200/100 and I’d put on more than 30 pounds in the last 3 weeks. They’ve run me through a huge number of tests over the last several days, meanwhile I’ve slept. I’ve been so exhausted and stressed out that I’ve either been sleeping or in the bathroom for most of the last 60+ hours. They’ve been changing my medication around trying to balance this failing house of cards, there’s my heart, fluid retention from my feet up to my mid chest, and now I’ve found out that my kidneys are functioning at 20% with as much as 10 grams of protein leaking into my urine!!! With the kidneys unable to take the stress of my massive fluid dump (3+ liters yesterday alone), they’ve decided to take me off the diuretics for now to give the twins a break, but the protein leakage is off the charts and no amount of BP medication seems to be able to get me below 167/77. The doctors have been punting the problem back and forth between departments, with no one wanting to take responsibility for my case. They stop by, say high, point fingers at another department and then leave. Today that changed, nephrology stopped by and for the first time a doctor sat down in my room and spent half an hour talking to me about my case and what she sees in my labs. She wanted to know what I’m eating at home, how my cold progressed and actually took the time to dig into my medical history for clues!
Nephrology believes that the sinus infection that I had in early October moved either to my heart or my kidneys. This caused fluid retention, causing the shortness of breath and high BP. They’ve taken more blood for lab tests about two hours ago and we’ll know more over the coming days, but nephrology doesn’t think I can get out of here until Tuesday or Wednesday at the earliest. This has already been a hard year, some ups, but a lot of downs (job loss, no insurance, ex-wife, etc.) and this just adds to it! I’m not scared for me, but I’m responsible for three little girls as their mom can barely take care of herself most days. I have a wonderful girlfriend who is amazing and has taken over all the house and most of the girl’s responsibilities. I’m scared for my girls, I need to be there for them to make sure they have an amazing childhood, a stable home, and are taught how to love and respect all people (something they won’t get from almost anyone else that might end up raising them)! Not much to do now but wait and scream into the void! Thanks for listening to me ramble and for keeping me laughing instead of crying throughout this.
item.dc:creator: Ranlothir

item.title: Unhinged dumping without a theme because everything sucks part two.
item.description:
My second post. I don't plan on doing this daily.
I plan on at least doing one dump a day on Sunday and perhaps other stuff sprinkled in.
Perhaps I'll do other stuff as well if people want it.
Anyway, enjoy the content that's been rotting on my phone for years (Which is why some of it will be older).
item.dc:creator: ShadeTc

item.title: The Squatter
item.dc:creator: zeroisnot

item.title: The original zero suit Samus
item.description:
For those too young to remember the original Metroid games, the green haired, pink/magenta leotard outfit was from the first Metroid game. If you entered your name as "Justin Bailey" it would let you play the game in that outfit. The name itself is kind of a secondary Easter Egg, since the word "bailey" is another word for swimsuit. So the name basically means "just in swimsuit". Cosplay by Phavorianne https://www.acparadise.com/acp/display.php?a=72
The black two piece outfit was originally a reward for beating the second Metroid game within a certain amount of time. At the end of the game you would get a screen showing Samus posing. If you beat the game within a certain time it would show her take off her helmet, and if you got an even better time it would show her without her armor, wearing this casual outfit of hers. (The outfit made appearances in later games with changes to the color and style.)
